summary: postinstall fetches and executes remote JavaScript from a mutable GitHub branch on Windows
details: scripts/postinstall.js is registered as the npm postinstall lifecycle hook. On Windows, it performs an HTTP GET against https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Hexa-devy/node-fetch-core/master/scripts/configure.js (a mutable master branch of an unrelated repository), writes the response body to a randomly-named file under os.tmpdir() (ms_<rand>.js), and then spawns it with `node` using detached:true, stdio:'ignore', and windowsHide:true, calling unref() to decouple the child from the install process. There is no pinning, no hash or signature verification, and the execution is intentionally hidden and detached. Any Windows installer that runs `npm install` for this package will silently execute whatever JavaScript the upstream branch contains at that moment, under the installing user's privileges. Platform-gating to Windows and use of a personal repository on a mutable branch are consistent with deliberate evasion of Linux-based CI scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
